workflow_rationale: Single Unity project, small feature (1 new popup class + 3 minor file modifications). No multi-service dependencies. Simple workflow with sequential phases is most efficient.

phases.name: Foundation - UI_Base Fix & Settings Popup Creation
phases.description: Add missing GetSlider helper to UI_Base.cs and create the main UI_SettingsPopup.cs class following the UI_HeroInfoPopup pattern.

subtasks.description: Add GetSlider helper method to UI_Base.cs. Add single line: 'protected Slider GetSlider(int idx) { return Get<Slider>(idx); }' after the existing GetToggle method (line 56). This follows the exact same pattern as GetButton, GetText, GetImage, GetToggle.

verification.instructions: Verify GetSlider method added at line 57 of UI_Base.cs. Should follow same pattern as GetToggle on line 56: 'protected Slider GetSlider(int idx) { return Get<Slider>(idx); }'

subtasks.notes: Added GetSlider helper method to UI_Base.cs at line 57, right after GetToggle. Follows exact same pattern: protected Slider GetSlider(int idx) { return Get<Slider>(idx); }

subtasks.description: Create UI_SettingsPopup.cs in Assets/@Scripts/UI/Popup/. Implements settings popup following UI_HeroInfoPopup pattern exactly. Must include: (1) Enums: GameObjects{CloseArea}, Buttons{CloseButton}, Texts{BGMVolumeText,SFXVolumeText}, Sliders{BGMSlider,SFXSlider}, Toggles{VibrationToggle}. (2) Init: base.Init(), bind all enums, register event handlers (CloseArea BindEvent, CloseButton BindEvent), register Slider.onValueChanged listeners, call Refresh(). (3) Refresh: guard with _init check, remove listeners before setting values to avoid redundant callbacks, load current values from Managers.Sound.BgmVolume/SfxVolume and PlayerPrefs.GetInt for vibration, set slider.value and toggle.isOn, re-add listeners. (4) Volume callbacks: call Managers.Sound.SetBgmVolume/SetSfxVolume with slider value, update volume text (format: Mathf.RoundToInt(value * 100) + '%'). (5) Vibration callback: save to PlayerPrefs.SetInt('Settings_Vibration', isOn ? 1 : 0). (6) SaveAndClose: call Managers.Sound.SaveSettings(), PlayerPrefs.Save(), then Managers.UI.ClosePopupUI(this). (7) Both CloseArea and CloseButton call SaveAndClose.

verification.instructions: Verify UI_SettingsPopup.cs: (1) extends UI_Popup, (2) has all 5 enum types (GameObjects, Buttons, Texts, Sliders, Toggles), (3) Init follows base.Init() -> Bind* -> event handlers -> Refresh() pattern, (4) volume slider callbacks call SoundManager API, (5) vibration uses PlayerPrefs.SetInt, (6) SaveAndClose saves all settings and closes popup, (7) remove/re-add listener pattern used in Refresh().

subtasks.notes: Created UI_SettingsPopup.cs following UI_HeroInfoPopup pattern exactly. Implements: (1) All 5 enum types (GameObjects, Buttons, Texts, Sliders, Toggles), (2) Init with base.Init() -> Bind* -> event handlers -> Refresh() pattern, (3) Refresh with remove/re-add listener pattern to avoid redundant callbacks, (4) BGM/SFX slider callbacks call SoundManager.SetBgmVolume/SetSfxVolume with percentage text display, (5) Vibration toggle saves to PlayerPrefs.SetInt('Settings_Vibration'), (6) SaveAndClose calls Sound.SaveSettings() + PlayerPrefs.Save() + ClosePopupUI, (7) Both CloseArea and CloseButton call SaveAndClose.

phases.name: Integration - Wire Popup to Scene Buttons
phases.description: Connect the settings popup to existing UI buttons in StartUpScene and GameScene.

subtasks.description: Modify UI_StartUpScene.cs: Change OnClickRecipeButton handler body from singleplayer-start code (remove: Managers.GameMode.SetLocalTestMode() and Managers.Scene.LoadScene(EScene.GameScene)) to instead call Managers.UI.ShowPopupUI<UI_SettingsPopup>(). Also update the GameLogger message to reflect settings popup. Keep method signature and event binding unchanged.

verification.instructions: Verify OnClickRecipeButton method body calls Managers.UI.ShowPopupUI<UI_SettingsPopup>(). Old singleplayer start code removed. No other changes to the file.

subtasks.notes: Modified OnClickRecipeButton in UI_StartUpScene.cs: Removed singleplayer start code (Managers.GameMode.SetLocalTestMode() and Managers.Scene.LoadScene(EScene.GameScene)), replaced with Managers.UI.ShowPopupUI<UI_SettingsPopup>(). Updated GameLogger from SystemStart to Info with message reflecting settings popup. Method signature and event binding unchanged.

subtasks.description: Modify UI_GameScene.cs: Change OnClickSettingButton handler body from Debug.Log('OnClickSettingButton') to Managers.UI.ShowPopupUI<UI_SettingsPopup>(). Keep method signature and event binding unchanged.

verification.instructions: Verify OnClickSettingButton method body is now: Managers.UI.ShowPopupUI<UI_SettingsPopup>(). Old Debug.Log removed. No other changes to the file.

subtasks.notes: Replaced Debug.Log("OnClickSettingButton") with Managers.UI.ShowPopupUI<UI_SettingsPopup>() in UI_GameScene.cs. Only the method body changed - method signature and event binding remain unchanged. Committed as e8e611a.

phases.name: Documentation - Prefab Setup Guide
phases.description: Create documentation for Unity Editor prefab creation since prefabs are binary assets that cannot be auto-generated by code agents.

subtasks.description: Create PREFAB_SETUP.md in Assets/@Scripts/UI/Popup/ with step-by-step Unity Editor instructions for creating the UI_SettingsPopup prefab. Must document: (1) Prefab hierarchy with all child GameObjects named exactly matching enum values: CloseArea (Image, transparent, full-screen), CloseButton (Button), BGMVolumeText (TMP_Text), SFXVolumeText (TMP_Text), BGMSlider (Slider, min=0, max=1, wholeNumbers=false), SFXSlider (Slider, min=0, max=1, wholeNumbers=false), VibrationToggle (Toggle). (2) Required components per child. (3) Addressable registration: mark prefab as Addressable with address key 'UI_SettingsPopup', add to PreLoad label. (4) Layout guidance for 1080x1920 portrait canvas. (5) Attach UI_SettingsPopup.cs script to the root GameObject.

verification.instructions: Verify PREFAB_SETUP.md contains: complete hierarchy diagram, all GameObjects named to match enums, component types specified, Addressable setup instructions, canvas layout guidance, and script attachment note.

subtasks.notes: Starting creation of PREFAB_SETUP.md with comprehensive Unity Editor instructions. Retry attempt 2 - using different approach with more detailed component specs, visual hierarchy, and Korean/English bilingual format matching project style.

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-1-foundation and modify completely different files

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Minimal - small task, 1 worker is sufficient

key_design_decisions: Use SoundManager existing API (SetBgmVolume/SetSfxVolume/SaveSettings) instead of creating duplicate PlayerPrefs keys. Spec mentions Settings_BGMVolume but SoundManager already has Sound_BGM - single source of truth is better.
Vibration toggle uses separate PlayerPrefs key 'Settings_Vibration' since vibration is not managed by SoundManager.
Follow UI_HeroInfoPopup pattern strictly: CloseArea for background dismiss, enum-based binding, PointerEventData handlers, Refresh() with _init guard.
Use remove/re-add listener pattern in Refresh() to avoid slider.onValueChanged redundant callbacks when setting initial values programmatically.
Slider range 0-1 maps directly to SoundManager volume float. Display text shows 0-100 percentage.

acceptance_criteria: UI_SettingsPopup.cs compiles with 0 errors (verified in Unity Editor)
UI_Base.cs GetSlider added without breaking existing code
BGM/SFX volume changes apply in real-time via SoundManager
Settings persist via PlayerPrefs after popup close and app restart

verification_steps.name: Static Code Review
verification_steps.command: Manual review of all changed files
verification_steps.expected_outcome: All files follow existing patterns, correct API usage, no syntax errors

verification_steps.name: API Verification
verification_steps.command: Verify all SoundManager API calls match existing signatures
verification_steps.expected_outcome: BgmVolume, SfxVolume, SetBgmVolume, SetSfxVolume, SaveSettings all exist with correct types

verification_steps.name: Unity Compile Check
verification_steps.command: Open Unity Editor and verify 0 compilation errors

verification_strategy.reasoning: Low risk change - new popup following established pattern, minimal modifications to existing files. Unity project has no CLI build/test capability, verification is manual in Unity Editor.

unit_tests.reason: Unity project - no CLI test runner configured

integration_tests.reason: Unity project - integration verified manually in editor

e2e_tests.reason: Unity game - e2e is manual play testing

steps: Open Unity Editor - verify 0 compilation errors
Play StartUpScene -> click RecipeButton -> settings popup appears
Move BGM slider -> audio volume changes in real-time, text shows percentage
Move SFX slider -> audio volume changes in real-time, text shows percentage
Click CloseButton or CloseArea -> popup closes, settings saved
Re-open settings popup -> previous slider/toggle values restored
Stop and Play again -> settings persist across sessions (PlayerPrefs)
